project_type: Static HTML/CSS/JS single-page application (no bundler, no framework)
build_system: None - pure static files loaded via script tags in index.html
test_framework: None - manual browser testing. For this task, we validate AST analysis via Node.js script
build_command: N/A (static HTML page, no build step)
test_command: NODE_OPTIONS='' node test-analysis.js (custom test script to validate the AST analysis engine)
verification_instructions: 1. Run the Node.js test script against the analysis engine to confirm it correctly detects issues in diverse code snippets. 2. Open index.html in a browser and paste code to confirm UI renders findings.
setup_instructions: No setup needed - static HTML/JS. ESLint 10.4.1 is available globally at /root/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/lib/node_modules/eslint/ with espree and acorn bundled.
environment_constraints: INTEGRATIONS_ONLY network mode. Cannot npm install. Cannot make real API calls to api.anthropic.com or api.openai.com from this sandbox. ESLint 10.4.1 is pre-installed globally (with espree/acorn). The code must be written to make real API calls that work when the user opens the page on their local machine. The AST analysis portion can be tested in-sandbox. For browser usage, a self-contained JS parser must be included since the browser won't have access to the Node.js ESLint global.
contribution_requirements: No formal contributing guide. Push to main branch.
key_patterns: Each JS module is a class (CrossVerify, Conversation, Companion, etc.) instantiated in app.js. Classes use init() method for DOM rendering. UI uses glass-card styling, panel layout with panel-header, CSS variables for theming. No module bundler - all classes are global.
relevant_files: index.html (script tags, Cross Verify panel section, inline styles)
directory_structure: / - index.html (main page with all inline styles), /js/ - 8 JS files (app.js orchestrator, companion.js, conversation.js, crossverify.js, dashboard.js, features.js, knowledge.js, terminal.js), /css/ - 3 CSS files (main.css, glassmorphism.css, animations.css)
